message:
feat(mux): add subscriber lifecycle management, synchronized output safety cap, and Rust 2024 fmt

Mux subscriber API improvements:
- Mux::subscribe() now returns a usize handle identifying the subscription
- New Mux::unsubscribe(sub_id) method removes a callback by handle, preventing
  stale subscribers from accumulating when domains are detached or sessions end

Synchronized output safety:
- Add MAX_HELD_SYNCHRONIZED_OUTPUT_BYTES (8 MiB) constant to bound memory when
  a buggy application enters synchronized-output mode without sending the reset
  sequence. parse_buffered_data() now force-flushes held actions once the buffer
  exceeds this limit, logging a warning rather than growing unboundedly

Tmux domain cleanup:
- TmuxDomainState now tracks its notification subscriber handle via
  notification_sub_id: Mutex<Option<usize>>
- subscribe_notification() is idempotent (early-returns if already subscribed)
  and validates the domain still exists before dispatching events
- unsubscribe_notification() cleanly removes the callback on session exit
- Exit handler now explicitly clears remote pane state, backlog, gui_tabs,
  pending_splits, tmux_session, and command queue before force-exiting tmux
  mode, then calls mux.domain_was_detached() to remove stale panes
- Uses Mux::try_get() instead of Mux::get() in exit path to avoid panics
  during shutdown

Formatting:
- cargo fmt under Rust 2024 edition reorders imports alphabetically across all
  mux source files (no semantic changes in those hunks)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
